title: Crack FAANG in 60 Days
description: A comprehensive FAANG interview prep covering advanced DSA, large-scale system design, and behavioral excellence.

steps.title: Programming Language Deep Dive
steps.description: Pick one language (Python/Java/JS) and master: closures, memory model, concurrency primitives, and runtime internals.

steps.title: DSA: Arrays, Strings & Hashing
steps.description: Two pointers, sliding window, frequency maps, anagram checks, string manipulation. Solve 10 problems.

steps.title: DSA: Linked Lists & Stacks/Queues
steps.description: Reverse linked list, detect cycle (Floyd's), merge sorted lists, LRU cache. Monotonic stack problems.

steps.title: DSA: Trees — BFS, DFS, BST
steps.description: Level order traversal, zigzag BFS, path sums, lowest common ancestor, validate BST, kth smallest.

steps.title: DSA: Heaps & Priority Queues
steps.description: Kth largest, merge K sorted lists, median of stream, task scheduler. Know heap sort.

steps.title: DSA: Graphs — Advanced
steps.description: Union-Find (DSU), Dijkstra, Bellman-Ford, topological sort, number of islands, clone graph.

steps.title: DSA: Dynamic Programming — Core Patterns
steps.description: Memoization vs tabulation. Patterns: Fibonacci, grid paths, longest subsequences, 0/1 knapsack, coin change.

steps.title: DSA: Backtracking
steps.description: Permutations, subsets, N-Queens, Sudoku solver, word search. Master the choose-explore-unchoose template.

steps.title: DSA: Binary Search & Advanced Patterns
steps.description: Binary search on answer space. Rotated array, search in 2D matrix, median of two sorted arrays.

steps.title: Core CS: Operating Systems
steps.description: Deep dive: process scheduling, virtual memory, deadlocks (Banker's algorithm), synchronization primitives.

steps.title: Core CS: Distributed Systems Fundamentals
steps.description: CAP theorem, consensus algorithms (Raft/Paxos basics), vector clocks, consistent hashing, replication strategies.

steps.title: SQL & Database Internals
steps.description: B-tree indexes, query execution plans, partitioning vs sharding, ACID vs BASE, database replication lag.

steps.title: System Design: Scale from 1 to 1 Million Users
steps.description: Start with a single server. Progressively add: load balancer, DB replica, CDN, cache, message queue, microservices.

steps.title: System Design: Design Google Search
steps.description: Web crawling, inverted index, PageRank concept, distributed indexing, query processing pipeline, caching.

steps.title: System Design: Design a Chat Application
steps.description: WebSockets vs long polling, message ordering guarantees, delivery receipts, offline message sync, E2E encryption overview.

steps.title: System Design: Design Netflix/YouTube
steps.description: Video ingestion pipeline, adaptive bitrate streaming (HLS/DASH), CDN edge caching, recommendation engine.

steps.title: System Design: Design a Distributed Cache
steps.description: Consistent hashing for key distribution, eviction policies (LRU/LFU), replication, Redis vs Memcached trade-offs.

steps.title: Amazon: Leadership Principles Deep Dive
steps.description: Prepare 2 STAR stories for each of the 16 LPs. Focus on: Customer Obsession, Ownership, Bias for Action, Invent & Simplify.

steps.title: Google: Algorithm Mindset & Clarification
steps.description: Practice asking clarifying questions before coding. Know Google's rubric: correctness, efficiency, readability, testing.

steps.title: Meta: Product Sense + System Design
steps.description: Meta combines system design with product thinking. Practice estimating scale and justifying technical choices by product impact.

steps.title: Mock DSA Round 1: Medium Problems
steps.description: Time yourself: solve 2 medium problems in 45 minutes. Explain your approach as you code (think out loud).

steps.title: Mock System Design Round
steps.description: Pick a system (Twitter feed, Uber, Dropbox) and design it from scratch in 45 minutes. Follow: requirements → scale estimates → high-level → deep dive.

steps.title: Mock DSA Round 2: Hard Problems
steps.description: Two hard-level DSA problems. Focus on optimal solutions — FAANG expects O(n log n) or better.

steps.title: Review Weak Areas
steps.description: Identify the 3 areas you're weakest in from your mock rounds. Spend 3 days drilling exactly those problem types.

steps.title: OOP & Low-Level Design
steps.description: Design patterns: Strategy, Observer, Factory, Decorator. Practice: design a parking lot, design an elevator system.

steps.title: Company Research: 5 Target Companies
steps.description: Read each company's question bank. Note their interview format, tech stack, and what they emphasize.

steps.title: Behavioral: Craft Your Final STAR Bank
steps.description: Finalize 5–7 STAR stories that cover: leadership, conflict, failure, technical achievement, cross-team impact.

steps.title: Full Mock Interview Day
steps.description: Simulate a full FAANG loop: 45 min DSA → 45 min system design → 30 min behavioral. With a peer if possible.

steps.title: Final Revision Pass
steps.description: Review your entire Revision List. Focus on explanations, not just solutions — interviewers want to hear your reasoning.

steps.title: Rest, Reflect & Trust the Process
steps.description: No new material today. Review your strengths. Go into interviews knowing you've put in the work. You're ready.
